intro: Pyrus hylanderi, also known as the Australian wild pear or wild pear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The Australian wild pear has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and fever, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus hylanderi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Pyrus hylanderi include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and digestive issues, as well as to support overall well-being.
